title: Sophia's Automotive AI Assessment - NADA Convention Edition
summary: This document is a template for Sophia's Automotive AI Assessment Scorecard, designed for objective vendor evaluation of alleged AI-powered products, particularly for the NADA Convention. It provides a structured framework to assess various aspects like AI authenticity, operational fit, and ROI clarity.
text:
Sophia's Automotive AI Assessment
NADA Convention Edition - Objective vendor evaluation for alleged AI-powered products
LIGHT MODE
Dealer Context (used to tailor rigor and recommendations)
Name / Store:
____________________________
Role / Title:
e.g., GM, Fixed Ops Director, Marketing, IT
Tenure (years in auto):
Tech adoption style:
Conservative / Pragmatic / Progressive / Innovative
Resources available:
Owner, IT, training time, integration support, budget
Vendor Snapshot
Vendor name:
Product / service:
Domain:
Fixed Ops / Variable Ops / Both
Core claim in 1 sentence:
What they say it does
What it replaces / improves:
Define Success (must be true in 12 months without the vendor)
Write outcomes in measurable terms (KPI + target + time):
Creator branding: #061635 (primary), #f7c906 (accent) on white. Dark mode: #1cbede + #f7c906 on black.
Page 1 of 2

Assessment Scorecard
Score each area 0-10. Weighted total = 0-100. Use notes to document vendor answers.
LIGHT MODE
Scoring (0-10 each)
Category
Weight
Score (0-10)
AI authenticity (not just automation)
20
Score:
Operational fit (process + people)
20
Score:
Resource alignment (time, IT, training)
15
Score:
Transparency & data control
15
Score:
ROI clarity (math, baselines, timeline)
15
Score:
Risk & exit strategy
10
Score:
Change management reality
5
Score:
Total Score (0-100)
Total:
Decision Guidance
80-100: Proceed with confidence  |  60-79: Proceed with guardrails  |  <60: High risk / hype detected
Notes (capture the vendor's answers + missing proof)
Tip: Ask for baselines, access to raw numbers, an exit plan, and measurable commitments in writing.
